properties.title: Mission Landing Zone AD + Entra One-Shot

options.text: Deploys MLZ core hub, Tier3 network, AD lab (1 DC, 2 member servers, 3 clients), Entra VM sign-in, and CSPM sample resources.

elements.name: identifier
elements.label: Resource Naming Identifier
elements.toolTip: 1-3 character identifier used in MLZ naming.

elements.name: workloadName
elements.label: Workload Name
elements.toolTip: 1-10 character workload name for Tier3 deployment.

elements.name: workloadShortName
elements.label: Workload Short Name
elements.toolTip: 1-3 alphanumeric characters used in Tier3 resource naming. Examples: 'id1' for identity, 't3' for tier3, 'ops' for operations. This appears in resource group and resource names.

validations.message: Use 1-3 alphanumeric characters (e.g. id1, t3, ops).

elements.name: prefix
elements.label: Resource Prefix
elements.toolTip: Prefix for VMs and CSPM resources.

elements.name: emailSecurityContact
elements.label: Security Contact Email
elements.toolTip: Email address for Defender for Cloud security alerts and recommendations.

options.text: Each Defender plan is billed separately. Review Azure Defender pricing before enabling plans in a production subscription.

elements.name: deployDefender
elements.label: Enable Defender for Cloud (base)
elements.toolTip: Enables the Defender for Cloud service and configures the security contact. Required before individual plans take effect.

elements.name: deployDefenderCspm
elements.label: Defender CSPM — Cloud Security Posture Management
elements.toolTip: Agentless posture management: attack path analysis, cloud security graph, agentless vulnerability assessment, and regulatory compliance dashboard. No per-VM agent required. Recommended for broad visibility.

elements.name: deployDefenderForServers
elements.label: Defender for Servers
elements.toolTip: Threat protection for Windows and Linux VMs. P1 includes Microsoft Defender for Endpoint (MDE) integration only. P2 adds agentless scanning, file integrity monitoring, JIT VM access, and adaptive application controls.

elements.name: defenderForServersSubPlan
elements.label: Defender for Servers Plan
elements.toolTip: P1: MDE integration only. P2: Full protection including vulnerability assessment, JIT VM access, and file integrity monitoring.

allowedValues.label: P1 — Defender for Endpoint integration only

elements.name: deployMde
elements.label: Onboard VMs to Microsoft Defender for Endpoint (MDE) at provisioning time
elements.toolTip: WARNING: Requires Defender for Servers (P1 or P2) enabled above AND Microsoft Defender for Cloud already configured in this subscription. Extension only installs on Windows Server VMs (DC + member servers) — Windows 11 clients are not supported. Do NOT enable in Azure Government unless Defender for Cloud is fully provisioned first.

elements.name: deployDefenderForStorage
elements.label: Defender for Storage
elements.toolTip: Detects malware uploads, anomalous access, and sensitive data exposure in Storage accounts. Priced per storage account.

elements.name: deployDefenderForSql
elements.label: Defender for SQL
elements.toolTip: Threat protection for SQL Server on Azure VMs and Arc-enabled machines. Detects SQL injection, brute force, and anomalous access.

elements.name: deployDefenderForContainers
elements.label: Defender for Containers
elements.toolTip: Protects AKS clusters and Azure Container Registry (ACR). Includes container image vulnerability assessment and run-time threat detection.

elements.name: deployDefenderForAppService
elements.label: Defender for App Service
elements.toolTip: Detects exploit attempts, malicious connections, and suspicious processes in App Service plans.

elements.name: deployDefenderForKeyVault
elements.label: Defender for Key Vault
elements.toolTip: Alerts on suspicious Key Vault access: TOR exit node access, unusual geolocation, high-volume secret enumeration, and credential theft patterns.

elements.name: deployDefenderForArm
elements.label: Defender for Resource Manager
elements.toolTip: Monitors all ARM management-plane operations for lateral movement, privilege escalation, suspicious tool usage, and cryptomining via automation accounts.

elements.name: deployDefenderForDns
elements.label: Defender for DNS
elements.toolTip: Detects DNS-based threats: data exfiltration via DNS tunneling, C2 beaconing, and malicious domain resolution from Azure resources.

elements.name: deployDefenderForOssDb
elements.label: Defender for Open-Source Relational Databases
elements.toolTip: Threat protection for Azure Database for PostgreSQL, MySQL, and MariaDB. Detects SQL injection and anomalous access.

elements.name: deployDefenderForCosmosDb
elements.label: Defender for Azure Cosmos DB
elements.toolTip: Detects SQL injection, anomalous access patterns, and potential data exfiltration from Cosmos DB accounts.

elements.name: deployDefenderForApis
elements.label: Defender for APIs
elements.toolTip: API security posture and runtime threat protection for APIs published through Azure API Management (APIM).

options.text: All initiatives are assigned in DoNotEnforce (audit-only) mode — they surface compliance gaps in the Defender for Cloud dashboard without blocking deployments. NIST 800-53 and IL5 are required for FedRAMP and DoD authorization packages.

elements.name: deployPolicy
elements.label: Apply MLZ Built-in Policy Set
elements.toolTip: Deploys Mission Landing Zone built-in policy assignments covering SCCA-aligned controls for hub/spoke networking, logging, and access management.

elements.name: deployNist80053
elements.label: NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5
elements.toolTip: Assigns the built-in NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5 regulatory compliance initiative. Covers 20 control families (AC, AU, CM, IA, SC, SI). Required for FedRAMP Moderate and High authorization.

elements.name: deployNist800171
elements.label: NIST SP 800-171 Rev. 2
elements.toolTip: Assigns the built-in NIST SP 800-171 Rev. 2 initiative for protection of Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI). Required for DFARS 252.204-7012 compliance and CMMC Level 2.

elements.name: deployIl5
elements.label: DoD Impact Level 5 (IL5) — Azure Government only
elements.toolTip: Assigns the DoD IL5 regulatory compliance initiative for National Security Systems and CUI up to SECRET. Only applicable in Azure Government regions.

elements.name: deployActivityLogDiagnosticSetting
elements.label: Send Activity Log to Log Analytics
elements.toolTip: Sends Azure subscription Activity Log (ARM deployments, RBAC changes, policy events, service health) to the MLZ Log Analytics workspace for long-term retention and querying.

elements.name: deploySentinel
elements.label: Enable Microsoft Sentinel
elements.toolTip: Deploys Microsoft Sentinel (cloud-native SIEM/SOAR) on a Log Analytics workspace. A new workspace will be created in the workload resource group unless you select an existing one.

elements.name: newWorkspaceName
elements.label: New workspace name
elements.toolTip: Name for the new Log Analytics workspace. Will be created in the workload tier3 resource group alongside the VMs.

constraints.validationMessage: 3–63 characters, letters/numbers/hyphens only, must start and end with a letter or number.

elements.name: existingWorkspace
elements.label: Existing Log Analytics Workspace
elements.toolTip: Select the Log Analytics workspace to enable Sentinel on.

options.text: This deployment includes CSPM/CWPP sample coverage resources (SQL DB, Storage + Blob, ACR, and a nested ARM deployment) useful for validating Defender alerts and compliance controls.

CSPM resource names are auto-generated from a deterministic hash of the workload resource group ID and the Prefix you set on the Basics tab. You cannot predict them before deployment, but they are stable across re-deploys.

Naming pattern (all in workload RG):
• Storage account: st[hash] (up to 24 chars)
• Blob container: coverage-container (always)
• Container Registry: acr[hash] (up to 50 chars)
• SQL Server: sql-[hash]
• SQL Database: defenderdb (always)

After deployment, find all names on the 'Outputs' tab of the deployment, or browse the workload resource group in the portal.
Azure Government does not support the Microsoft.Graph ARM provider. Auto-create Entra security group is not available. Provide an existing Entra principal Object ID below.

elements.name: autoCreateEntraGroup
elements.label: Auto-create a new Entra security group for VM access
elements.toolTip: Creates a new Entra ID security group during deployment and assigns it VM Login access on all VMs. Requires the deployment service principal to have Microsoft Graph group creation permissions (GroupMember.ReadWrite.All). Not available in Azure Government.

options.text: A new security group will be created in Entra ID and assigned the VM Administrator Login role on all deployed VMs. After deployment, add users to this group in the Azure Portal: Microsoft Entra ID > Groups > find the group > Members > Add.

elements.name: entraGroupName
elements.label: New Security Group Name
elements.toolTip: Display name for the new Entra security group. Will default to mlz-{prefix}-vm-users if left blank.

elements.name: entraLoginPrincipalId
elements.label: Entra Principal Object ID
elements.toolTip: Object ID (GUID) of an existing Entra ID user, group, or service principal for VM Login access. Find it: Azure Portal > Microsoft Entra ID > Users or Groups > select your account/group > copy Object ID. Using a security group is recommended — add/remove members without redeploying.

validations.message: Enter a valid GUID in the format xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx.

elements.name: entraPrincipalType
elements.label: Entra Principal Type
elements.defaultValue: Group (recommended — assign access to a security group)
elements.toolTip: Must match the type of the Object ID above. Group = security group (add/remove members without redeploying). User = individual Entra ID account. ServicePrincipal = app registration or managed identity.

allowedValues.label: Group (recommended — assign access to a security group)
Service Principal (app registration or managed identity)

elements.name: assignVmAdminRole
elements.label: Assign Virtual Machine Administrator Login role

elements.name: assignVmUserRole
elements.label: Assign Virtual Machine User Login role

elements.name: serverImageSku
elements.label: Server Image SKU
elements.toolTip: Marketplace server image SKU. Windows Server 2022 Gen2 is recommended for most deployments.

elements.name: serverImageVersion
elements.label: Server Image Version
elements.toolTip: Use 'latest' or provide a specific marketplace version.

elements.name: serverVmSize
elements.label: Server VM Size
elements.toolTip: VM size for the domain controller and member servers. Standard_D4ads_v6 is recommended and available in Azure Government regions.

allowedValues.label: Standard_D2ads_v6 (2 vCPUs, 8 GiB) — small lab / dev
Standard_D4s_v5 (4 vCPUs, 16 GiB) — Azure Commercial only
Standard_D2s_v5 (2 vCPUs, 8 GiB) — Azure Commercial only

elements.name: clientImageSku
elements.label: Client Image SKU
elements.toolTip: Marketplace client image SKU. 24H2 is the current Windows 11 release and recommended for Azure Government.

constraints.allowedValues: [if(equals(steps('remoteAccess').clientImageOffer, 'windows-10'), parse('[{"label":"Windows 10 Pro 22H2","value":"win10-22h2-pro"},{"label":"Windows 10 Enterprise 22H2","value":"win10-22h2-ent"}]'), parse('[{"label":"Windows 11 Pro 24H2 (recommended)","value":"win11-24h2-pro"},{"label":"Windows 11 Enterprise 24H2","value":"win11-24h2-ent"},{"label":"Windows 11 Pro 23H2","value":"win11-23h2-pro"},{"label":"Windows 11 Enterprise 23H2","value":"win11-23h2-ent"}]'))]

elements.name: clientImageVersion
elements.label: Client Image Version
elements.toolTip: Use 'latest' or provide a specific marketplace version.

elements.name: clientVmSize
elements.label: Client VM Size
elements.toolTip: VM size for the 3 Windows client workstations. Standard_D4ads_v6 is recommended and available in Azure Government regions.

allowedValues.label: Standard_D2ads_v6 (2 vCPUs, 8 GiB) — small lab / dev
Standard_D4s_v5 (4 vCPUs, 16 GiB) — Azure Commercial only
Standard_D2s_v5 (2 vCPUs, 8 GiB) — Azure Commercial only

elements.name: deployLinuxVm
elements.label: Deploy a Linux VM into the workload subnet
elements.toolTip: Deploys one additional Linux VM (prefix-lnx01) into the same Tier3 subnet. Gets Entra SSH login and, if MDE onboarding is enabled on the Security tab, the MDE.Linux extension. No domain join — Linux VM is standalone.

options.text: The Linux VM requires outbound internet access (HTTP/HTTPS to apt and packages.microsoft.com) to install the Entra SSH login extension. In MLZ deployments, all spoke traffic routes through Azure Firewall which may block these requests. If the extension fails, the entire deployment will fail. Only enable this if your Azure Firewall policy allows outbound internet access from the spoke subnet.

elements.name: linuxFlavor
elements.label: Linux Distribution
elements.toolTip: Select the Linux distro. Ubuntu is strongly recommended — it installs the AAD SSH extension via apt which works in isolated/gov networks. RHEL 9 requires RHUI repo access and will fail if DNS or outbound connectivity to rhui4-1.microsoft.com is unavailable at extension install time.

allowedValues.label: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 (not recommended — requires RHUI access)

elements.name: linuxVmSize
elements.label: Linux VM Size
elements.toolTip: VM size for the Linux VM. Standard_D2ads_v6 is available in Azure Government regions.

allowedValues.label: Standard_D2s_v5 (2 vCPUs, 8 GiB) — Azure Commercial only
Standard_D4s_v5 (4 vCPUs, 16 GiB) — Azure Commercial only
